user_query:
Please adjudicate the raw memory records in input/records.json and produce a human-readable report and a structured JSON result.

Requirements:
- Read and validate the JSON array from input/records.json. Treat each element as a memory record with fields: id (string), kind (fact|goal|speculation), entity (string), content (string), timestamp (ISO 8601), provenance ({sourceId, sourceLabel, capturedAt}), and optional tags (string[]).
- Enforce schema conformance. Normalize kind to lowercase enum values. Any malformed records should not be dropped silently; collect them under a schema_errors array in the output with the record id and a short reason.
- Deduplicate near-duplicate records using Jaccard word-overlap similarity at a fixed 0.8 threshold on the content field. Merge duplicates into a single canonical record that:
  - preserves a single canonical id (choose one) and includes mergedFrom (array of input ids merged into it, excluding the canonical id),
  - preserves all source provenance by emitting provenance as an array of objects (sourceId, sourceLabel, capturedAt) with duplicates removed.
- Resolve naming conflicts where the same real-world entity appears under multiple aliases (e.g., spelling or casing variants). Choose a canonical entity name and produce an entity_canonical_map mapping canonical name -> array of aliases observed (including the canonical if helpful). Update records to use the canonical entity.
- Archive stale records older than 30 days (fixed TTL) based on the timestamp field. Do not delete them; move them to an archived array in the result. All remaining, non-archived, valid records belong in active.
- Detect contradictions among active records using a heuristic approach (negation proximity and numeric/date divergence are sufficient). Output a contradictions array with entries listing ids (two record ids) and a brief reason.
- Preserve provenance and include it for every active and archived record as an array.

Deliverables:
- Write a concise markdown adjudication report to output/report.md summarizing: total input records, valid vs invalid counts, number of deduplications (and which ids were merged), naming conflict resolutions (canonical -> aliases), number archived due to TTL (30 days), and any contradictions found (with brief reasons). The report should explicitly mention the fixed Jaccard threshold "0.8" and the TTL "30 days" used.
- Write a structured JSON result to output/result.json with this top-level shape:
  {
    "active": [ ...records... ],
    "archived": [ ...records... ],
    "entity_canonical_map": { "Canonical Name": ["Alias A", "Alias B", ...], ... },
    "contradictions": [ { "ids": ["id1", "id2"], "reason": "..." }, ... ],
    "schema_errors": [ { "id": "...", "reason": "..." }, ... ]
  }
Where each record in active/archived has: id, kind (fact|goal|speculation), entity (canonicalized), content, timestamp (ISO 8601), provenance (array of {sourceId, sourceLabel, capturedAt}), optional tags (string[]), and optional mergedFrom (array of ids).

Please ensure:
- All kinds are one of fact|goal|speculation in lowercase.
- At least one deduplication is performed (with mergedFrom populated and combined provenance kept).
- At least one entity alias set is resolved in entity_canonical_map.
- At least one stale record is archived due to being older than 30 days.
- At least one contradiction is identified and listed.

Save outputs to:
- output/report.md
- output/result.json
